old_claim.note: Nanotechnology (Early Research) is an earlier historical predecessor or foundation, not a one-to-one engineering dependency.

new_claim_absent: No direct dependency remains because shape-memory alloys are metallurgy/materials technologies rather than technologies requiring the later nanotechnology field label.

source_shape.source_locator: Nanotechnology history review describing the later emergence of nanotechnology terminology and field identity.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The review establishes nanotechnology as a named field later than the broad postwar alloy-development era represented by this node.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Shape-memory behavior is modeled better through metallurgy and clinical application context; nanotechnology may support later characterization but is too narrow as a prerequisite.

ontology_before: Shape-memory alloys inherited a later nanotechnology predecessor edge that conflated nanoscale explanation with required technology lineage.
ontology_after: Shape-memory alloys now depend on metallurgy and clinical medicine only, pending a more precise alloy-history source pass.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Graph invariant 2026-06-07-semiconductor-energy-agriculture-chronology.json keeps this removed edge absent and documents the corrected dependency scope.
would_reject_if: The node were narrowed to mean nanoscale-engineered shape-memory alloy devices.
A stronger shape-memory alloy history source showed nanotechnology was prerequisite to the scoped technology.
A new materials-characterization node made the dependency precise and chronologically valid.
short_reason: Shape-memory alloys should not require the nanotechnology field label.
